summary: All 7 user-reported bugs verified fixed end-to-end. Backend: 15/15 pytest cases pass after I fixed one CRITICAL server.py ordering bug — `app.include_router(api)` was called BEFORE `@api.post('/upload')` was defined, so /api/upload returned 404 for every request (uploads completely broken). Moved include_router to after the upload route was registered; sudo supervisorctl restart backend; re-ran full suite → 15/15 green. Photo URLs: Lagos entry now serves the Wikipedia 'Lagos_pedestrial_area,_Algarve,_Portugal.JPG' (Portugal, NOT Pisa) and Lisboa serves 'Exterior_da_estação_de_oriente.jpg' (Gare do Oriente), both HTTP 200 via a browser User-Agent with 2 redirects each, content-type image/*. Frontend (mobile 390x844): diary/Lisboa detail page shows hero photo of Gare do Oriente, meta row: '305 km • ≈ 4 h • Brisa quente' — the computed travel time (Math.max(1, Math.round(305/80)) = 4) renders with the clock icon exactly as specified, and no '(?)' appears anywhere in the meta. Lagos detail's hero photo is the Wikipedia Lagos pedestrian area (verified by scraping src attributes). The i18n rename is correct ('Meteorologia (opcional)' PT / 'Weather (optional)' EN). diary/new.tsx uses `date: ""` — grep confirmed the only occurrence is `date: ""` (no bare `date,`). Places admin PUT works — the backend test PUTs a place without a `date` field and gets 200 + GET-verified persistence. Uploads: POST /api/upload accepts multipart PNG, stores under /app/backend/uploads/, returns {url,kind,filename}, and the file is retrievable via /api/uploads/; requires auth (401/403 without token); rejects .exe with 400. No AI service is called in the upload path — server.py has zero OpenAI/emergent-LLM references. countries.ts spot-check: FR uses Lyon (pexels-photo-2901214) — not Paris/Eiffel.
action_items: (Already fixed by testing agent) Route registration order in /app/backend/server.py — /api/upload must be declared BEFORE app.include_router(api). This has been corrected and backend restarted. Please keep this ordering when editing server.py in the future.
Optional cosmetic: passlib bcrypt version warning at startup (AttributeError: module 'bcrypt' has no attribute '__about__') — harmless, pinned in previous iteration notes. Not blocking.
Optional cosmetic: expo warns about outdated versions (expo@54.0.34, expo-font@14.0.11, expo-router@6.0.23). Not blocking any flow.
Optional: expo-secure-store on web is in-memory — a full page reload logs the user out. Already noted in iteration_3.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: FIXED — moved `app.include_router(api)` from line 467 to AFTER the /upload route declaration. Previously the upload route was registered on the APIRouter but the router had already been included in the app, so FastAPI never picked up the new route → 404. Uploads endpoint fully broken until this fix.

server.py: POST /api/upload correctly uses shutil.copyfileobj to write to /app/backend/uploads. No OpenAI, emergent LLM, or any AI integration references anywhere in the upload path (grep-verified). Confirmed no AI credits are consumed.
server.py places PUT endpoint accepts PlaceIn model which does NOT include any `date` field — user's 'Property date doesn't exist' error is resolved at the model layer.
diary/[id].tsx line 66: correctly renders `<Meta icon='clock' text={`≈ ${Math.max(1, Math.round(entry.distance_km/80))} h`} />` when distance_km > 0, ensuring a valid travel time always shows.

diary/[id].tsx line 67: weather is hidden if /\?/.test(entry.weather) — the '4h(?)' string containing '?' would be filtered out, but the seeded weather values ('Sol, 24 °C', 'Brisa quente', 'Nublado') contain no '?' so they render correctly.
diary/new.tsx line 38: `date: ""` in the POST body — the runtime error 'Property date doesn't exist' can no longer trigger from this file.
i18n.ts: pt.new_weather = 'Meteorologia (opcional)', en.new_weather = 'Weather (optional)' — confirmed on lines 52 and 114.

admin/place/[id].tsx: PUT payload builds cleanly from state and place — no `date` field is ever sent. Save button has testID='save'. All photo/video/description/experience/distance fields present with proper testIDs.
updated_files: /app/backend/server.py (moved app.include_router(api) to AFTER the /api/upload route so it is actually registered; also moved the /api/uploads static mount below include_router for clarity)
/app/backend/tests/test_alberto_bugfix_iter4.py (new — 15 regression tests: 6 photos, 2 places PUT, 3 uploads, 2 auth, 2 entries CRUD)

success_rate.frontend: 100% (Lisboa detail meta + hero photo + Lagos hero photo all verified in UI)

seed_data_creation: None persisted. Test suite creates a TEST_new_entry, verifies via GET, then deletes and re-verifies 404. Also PUTs a place with 'TEST_edit description' and restores the original description in teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 4 iterations of bug fixes verified. Backend URL: https://alberto-rails.preview.emergentagent.com. Credentials: alberto / lagos2026. Regression suites: /app/backend/tests/test_auth_login.py (5), /app/backend/tests/test_alberto_api.py, /app/backend/tests/test_alberto_bugfix.py, /app/backend/tests/test_alberto_bugfix_iter4.py (15). CRITICAL: if any new /api/* route is added, ensure @api.<method>() is declared BEFORE `app.include_router(api)` in server.py — otherwise the route silently 404s. Uploads land in /app/backend/uploads/ and are served at /api/uploads/<filename>. The upload endpoint requires Authorization: Bearer <token>. Photos in seeded entries are: Lagos → wikimedia Lagos_pedestrial_area, Lisboa → wikimedia Exterior_da_estação_de_oriente, Lyon → pexels 2901214.
rca_of_issue: Root cause of the /api/upload 404: `app.include_router(api)` was called on line 467 of server.py, but @api.post('/upload') was declared on line 476 — AFTER the router had been included. FastAPI's include_router copies the routes present on the router at the moment of the call; routes added to the router afterwards are NOT propagated. This meant every upload attempt returned 404 Not Found. Fix: moved `app.include_router(api)` to line 490 (after all @api decorators including /upload); moved the StaticFiles mount for /api/uploads just below it (mounts on `app` directly so its position doesn't affect registration, kept together for readability). Restart backend → /api/upload returns 200 with {url, kind, filename}.
